architecture.summary: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) is a four-stage deliberation method where a language model first drafts an initial response, then plans verification questions to fact-check the draft, answers those questions independently to avoid bias from prior responses, and finally generates a verified final response. Information flows through text prompting: the model's own generation at each stage informs the next, with verification questions and independent answers used to constrain and correct the final output.

biblio.title: Chain-of-Verification Reduces Hallucination in Large Language Models

claims.statement: COVE decreases hallucinations across a variety of tasks, from list-based questions from Wikidata, closed book Multi-SpanQA and longform text generation
